content.full_text:
County of San Bernardino

COUNTY COUNSEL
County Government Center
385 North Arrowhead Avenue
San Bernardino, CA 92415-0140
(714) 387-5455
Telecopier (714) 387-5462

March 4, 1991

John Wallace, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Ethics in Government Act of 1990 (Govt. Code §89500 et seq.)

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This letter will serve to confirm our telephone conversation of March 1, 1991, in which you kindly provided me with assistance interpreting the Ethics in Government Act of 1990, Government Code Sections 89500 et seq. Based upon our conversation, it is my understanding that your department is interpreting the Act, with respect to local elected office holders as follows.

The only section within that Act which expressly places limitations upon local elected office holders is Section 89501. The balance of the Act refers to elected state officers and would not apply to local elected officers. Specifically, the restrictions and exceptions outlined in Section 89506 with respect to travel do not apply to local elected office holders. Thus, as is stated in Section 89501, no elected office holder may accept any honoraria or any gift from any single source which is in excess of $1,000 in any calendar year. The last phrase of Section 89501, however, excepts from the $1,000 limit reimbursement for actual travel expenses and reasonable subsistence in connection therewith. It is my understanding that you interpret the language "in connection therewith" to refer back to the restriction regarding honoraria, but not gifts. So, any reimbursement for actual travel expenses and subsistence would be excepted from the thousand dollar limit on honoraria, but the thousand dollar limit on gifts include any gifts of travel expenses.

During our conversation, you indicated to me that the Commission will soon be proposing regulations to implement these sections. If I am not already on your mailing list for proposed regulations, I would certainly like to be placed on it.

Thank you for your cooperation and your prompt response to my inquiry. If I have misunderstood or misstated your advice regarding these sections, please let me know at your earliest convenience.

Very truly yours,

ALAN K. MARKS
County Counsel

W. ANDREW HARTZELL
Deputy County Counsel

WAH:mar/Wallace
